---
description: "Language policy for this project: always respond in English, write code in English, and localize end-user text to Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR)."
applyTo: "**"
---
# Language And Localization Rules

## Assistant Response Language
- Write all assistant explanations and guidance in English.

## Code Language
- Generate source code in English.
- Use English for identifiers (variables, functions, classes, types, constants, file names).
- Use English for code comments and documentation written for developers.

## Documentation Language
- Write project documentation in English by default (README, requirements, technical docs).

## User-Facing Language
- Write all user-facing text in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) with proper accentuation.
- This includes UI labels, placeholders, validation messages, alerts, notifications, and frontend fallback API error messages.

## Precedence
- If text is shown to end users, use pt-BR.
- If text is for developers or implementation details, use English.
